methods.description: Process for extracting and preparing lake sediment samples for further analysis. The sediment was collected from the deepest point of the lake using a sonar device to determine the location. A drive-operated surface corer was used to extract the sediment, which was then divided into subsamples based on texture and color.

steps.description: The lake sediment was taken at the deepest point in the lake (12 m), determined by a sonar device (Plastimo Echotest II).
steps.searchable_summary: determine deepest point of lake using sonar device

steps.description: The aluminum platform, attached to the lake bank at five spots, was used.
steps.searchable_summary: set up aluminum platform for sediment extraction

steps.description: To remove a lake sediment core, a drive-operated surface corer with clear polycarbonate of 1-m long tubes, was used.
steps.searchable_summary: extract sediment core using surface corer

steps.description: Sediment of an overall length of 11.44 m was extracted. Because of the pressure that the corer makes, sediment core was compressed for a 0.5 m.
steps.searchable_summary: account for sediment compression during extraction

steps.description: Each tube consisted of two halves, divided along the length, which permitted division of the core sample into two halves as well.
steps.searchable_summary: divide sediment core into two halves

steps.description: In the laboratory, the core was split into subsamples according to their texture and color.
steps.searchable_summary: split core into subsamples based on texture and color

steps.description: Splitting the sample into subsamples resulted in 95 subsamples.
steps.searchable_summary: count and record number of subsamples
